query: Has Russia announced any extension of its gasoline (motor gasoline/benzine) export ban beyond July 31, 2026? Look for any cabinet resolution, decree, or official statement from late June 2026 or early July 2026 reported by TASS, Reuters, or government.ru. Also describe the recent status of Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian refineries and domestic gasoline shortages as of late June 2026, and historically how many days before each deadline past gasoline export ban extensions were formally announced.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Direct Answer

**No — as of July 1–2, 2026, Russia had NOT formally announced or signed any decree extending its gasoline export ban beyond July 31, 2026.** The government has *publicly signaled intent* (on June 23 it said it is "seriously considering" extending gasoline/diesel/jet-fuel bans to end-2026), but no binding cabinet resolution had been enacted.

**Note on distinct measures that WERE signed in late June** (easy to confuse with the ban):
- June 26: Putin extended the *price-cap countermeasure* ban to Dec 31, 2027
- June 25: A *sulfur* export ban extended to end-2026
- ~June 28: Request to the EEC to extend the zero gasoline export *duty* — none of these is the gasoline export ban itself.

## Historical timing pattern
Russia enacts these extensions **at the last minute — typically 0–5 days before the deadline**:
- Aug 31, 2025 → announced Aug 27 (~4 days)
- Sep 30, 2025 → decree Sep 30 (same day)
- Dec 31, 2025 → announced ~Dec 27 (~4 days)

This pattern implies a July 31, 2026 extension would historically be expected around ~July 27–31 — fully consistent with no decree existing yet on July 1.

## Drone strikes & shortages (late June 2026)
- Moscow (Kapotnya) refinery hit June 16 & 18; TANECO offline after June 12; Krasnodar/Yaroslavl targeted
- ~25–30% of refining capacity offline; throughput below 4 mbd in early June (21-year low); June gasoline output –15% y/y
- Rationing (20–30 L/person) in 24+ regions (up to 53–55 counting private/occupied limits); Crimea halted retail sales June 21
- Putin called it "difficult but not critical"; Kremlin confirmed talks to import fuel

Full report and structured data (key_facts, sources, data_points) have been submitted.

sources.title: Ban on gasoline exports from Russia extended to producers of petroleum products
sources.snippet: The Russian government has extended gasoline export restrictions effective until July 31, 2026, to direct producers of petroleum products.

sources.title: Russia Bans Producers From Exporting Gasoline Until July 31
sources.snippet: A new decree now extends the ban to producers, with exemptions granted only under intergovernmental agreements.

sources.title: Russia studies extending ban on gasoline and diesel exports (to end of 2026)
sources.snippet: The Russian government announced June 23 it is seriously considering extending the ban on exports of gasoline, diesel, and jet fuel until the end of the year.

sources.title: Putin held a meeting on supplying fuel to the domestic market
sources.snippet: The government has extended the export ban on gasoline and diesel until July 31, 2026; Novak said the situation is 'difficult but controlled'.

sources.title: Putin extended retaliatory measures on the oil price cap (to Dec 31, 2027)
sources.snippet: Putin signed a decree extending Russia's retaliatory ban on exports under the Western price cap until December 31, 2027; Novak considering a diesel export ban.

sources.title: Russia applied to EAEU to extend zero gasoline export duty
sources.snippet: Novak: zero export duty on gasoline until July 1 is being extended; proposal sent to Eurasian Economic Commission, decision expected next week.

sources.title: Russian government extends ban on gasoline exports until September 30, 2025
sources.snippet: MOSCOW, August 27. Government extends the temporary ban on gasoline exports for all participants until September 30, 2025; non-producers until October 31.

sources.title: Russia Extends Gasoline Export Ban, Restricts Diesel Shipments
sources.snippet: Government said Tuesday (Sept 30) the new decree prolongs the gasoline export ban until Dec. 31 (government.ru news 56377).

sources.title: Russia Extends Temporary Gasoline-Export Ban Through February
sources.snippet: Russia extended the gasoline export ban through February (announced ~Dec 27, 2025).

sources.title: Even in Moscow no gasoline: Putin sees problems for oil nation Russia
sources.snippet: ~30% of refining capacity offline; Q2 throughput -11% to ~4.7 mbd; June gasoline output -15% y/y; Putin admits drone attacks cause problems but 'not critical'.

sources.title: Russia lives its worst fuel shortage since 2009 after Ukrainian drone strikes
sources.snippet: 20+ strikes on Russian oil infrastructure in 2026, >$7bn losses; >20% capacity out; restrictions in 20+ regions (55 of 83 with private limits).

sources.title: Ukrainian drones attacked Moscow: key refinery of the RF capital hit
sources.snippet: June 18 drone strike on Moscow (Kapotnya) refinery following June 16 strike; supplies 40% of Moscow gasoline, 50% diesel; limits in 53 regions.

sources.title: Fuel Catastrophe: Ukrainian Armed Forces vs. Russian Economy
sources.snippet: TANECO (Nizhnekamsk) offline after June 12 strike; refining below 4 mbd in early June (lowest in 21 years); shortages in 53 regions.

sources.title: Fuel crisis in Russia
sources.snippet: ~25% of refining capacity inoperative (Reuters); restrictions in 24 regions; Crimea retail sales halted; Putin (June 28) calls it a 'difficult stage'.

sources.title: What is happening with gasoline in Moscow on July 1, 2026: new rules, limits, prices
sources.snippet: July 1 prices: AI-92 64.67-65, AI-95 71.46-75 (up to 86); export ban extended until July 31, 2026; new prepayment/jerrycan rules.

sources.title: Putin announced a possible ban on diesel fuel exports
sources.snippet: June 28: Putin evaluating full diesel export ban; gasoline reserves 1.7 million tons (only 4% below year-ago).

sources.title: Russia is negotiating imports of petroleum products - Kremlin
sources.snippet: June 30: Peskov confirms talks to import fuel; export ban extended until July 31; Crimea sales to individuals halted since June 21.

sources.title: 2025-2026 Russian fuel crisis
sources.snippet: Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian refineries since August 2025 caused widespread damage and a fuel crisis.

key_facts: As of July 1-2, 2026, NO formal decree extending the gasoline export ban beyond July 31, 2026 had been signed or announced by TASS, Reuters, or government.ru.
The current gasoline export ban expires July 31, 2026 (set by the April 2, 2026 decree, government.ru news 58316, extending it to producers).

On June 23, 2026 the Russian government publicly stated it is 'seriously considering' extending gasoline/diesel/jet fuel export bans to the end of 2026 to avoid local shortages (intent signaled, not enacted).
June 26, 2026: Putin signed a decree extending the price-cap COUNTERMEASURE ban to Dec 31, 2027 (a distinct measure, not the gasoline export ban).
~June 28, 2026: Novak said Russia submitted a request to the Eurasian Economic Commission to extend the zero gasoline export DUTY (expiring July 1) to end-2026, decision expected 'next week'.
Historical pattern: extensions announced very close to deadline - Aug 27, 2025 (~4 days before Aug 31); Sep 30, 2025 same-day (government.ru 56377); ~Dec 27, 2025 (~4 days before Dec 31).

Ukrainian drones struck the Moscow (Kapotnya/Gazprom Neft) refinery on June 16 and June 18, 2026, damaging the primary AVT distillation unit; it supplies ~40% of Moscow's gasoline and ~50% of its diesel.
TANECO refinery (Nizhnekamsk) ceased operations after a June 12, 2026 drone attack; refineries in Krasnodar and Yaroslavl also targeted.

Estimated 25-30% of Russian refining capacity was offline (Reuters ~25%, Carnegie ~30%); Q2 2026 throughput fell ~11% to ~4.7 million bpd; volumes dropped below 4 mbd in early June (lowest in 21 years).

Gasoline sales rationing (20-30 liters/person) imposed across 24+ official regions (up to 53-55 counting private/occupied-territory limits); Crimea halted retail fuel sales to individuals from June 21, 2026.
July 1, 2026 prices: AI-92 ~64.67-65 rubles/L, AI-95 ~71.46-75 (up to 86), premium >95-100+, diesel ~78.49-80 rubles/L; wholesale AI-95 up ~33% in June.
Putin (June 28-29) acknowledged drone strikes 'create problems' but called the situation 'not critical'; Kremlin (Peskov, June 30) confirmed talks to import petroleum products.
Reported gasoline reserves stood at 1.7 million tons as of June 28, only ~4% below the same period last year (Energy Ministry data cited by Putin).
